annotations.title: Inebriated.
annotations.text: <mn>◦</mn> Not that they did eat or drink too much, or fell into excess, but competently. As the earth is said to be inebriated with rain (Psal. 64.) being sufficiently watered, and so made fruitful, not drowned, nor overflowed, for so it is unfruitful. S. Hierome Tradit. Heb. S. Aug. q. 144. super Gen.

notes.text: Moderation to be used in feasting.
